Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given two logs Log A and Log B both for Order1234

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: We will align events by order_id and event timing with a tolerance of 2 seconds

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We will map event types between the two logs Log A Order Received to Log B OrderReceived Log A Order Validated to Log B OrderValidation Log A Payment Processed to Log B PaymentCheck Log A Item Shipped to Log B Shipping Log B has an extra event Quality Check which is not in Log A

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We will create a merged event log that includes a common timestamp we can choose the primary as Log As timestamp and note Log Bs if needed

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will choose Log As timestamp as the primary for ordering and display but store Log Bs in a separate attribute

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We will create a new timestamp field for the merged event that is the Log A timestamp if present or Log B timestamp if only Log B present and then have a field for the other timestamp

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: For merged events we will combine all attributes

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: We will create a common event name by mapping Order Received and OrderReceived to Order Received Order Validated and OrderValidation to Order Validated Payment Processed and PaymentCheck to Payment Processed Item Shipped and Shipping to Item Shipped

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The requirement is to leave them separate if not matched so we will have one event for Quality Check that only comes from Log B

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We will create a merged log with columns order_id event_type_A event_type_B merged_event_name timestamp_A timestamp_B user_id resource_id notes and source

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: For each event in Log A sorted by timestamp look for an event in Log B for the same order_id that is within 2 seconds and not already matched if found merge otherwise keep separate then add unmatched events from Log B

Name: Validation - PE
Text: We must avoid matching the same Log B event to two Log A events so we need to mark events as matched

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We will sort the merged events by the primary timestamp using timestamp_A if available otherwise timestamp_B

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Event A1 Order Received at 100000 compared with B1 095958 difference 2 seconds matches

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Event A2 Order Validated at 100130 compared with B2 100129 difference 1 second matches

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Event A3 Payment Processed at 100200 compared with B3 100205 difference 5 seconds does not match

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Event A4 Item Shipped at 100500 compared with B5 100502 difference 2 seconds matches

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Event A5 Item Delivered at 102000 has no match in Log B

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Matched pairs A1 with B1 A2 with B2 A4 with B5 Unmatched from A A3 and A5 Unmatched from B B3 and B4

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The sorted order is 100000 merged A1 B1 100130 merged A2 B2 100200 A3 only 100205 B3 only 100300 B4 only 100500 merged A4 B5 102000 A5 only

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The event at 100200 from A and the event at 100205 from B are separate because they are 5 seconds apart and we used a tolerance of 2 seconds inclusive

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: We also have the Quality Check event from Log B at 100300 that is not in Log A and the Item Delivered event in Log A at 102000 that is not in Log B
